index.name: NYSE FANG+ Index
index.description: NYSE FANG+ is a global first — a 10-stock equal-weight index of the world's most influential technology and consumer internet companies. Unlike Nifty 50's free-float cap-weight methodology, equal weighting (≈10% each) ensures no single company dominates returns, rebalanced quarterly. An Indian investor buying MAFANG owns fractional exposure to NVIDIA's AI supremacy, Meta's 3.2B daily active users, Apple's $3T ecosystem, Amazon's cloud + logistics dominance, and Alphabet's search monopoly — all in a single NSE-listed ETF denominated in rupees. The embedded currency factor is a key feature: when USD strengthens vs INR (as it has 5–6% annually on long-run average), this ETF gains purely from INR depreciation on top of USD price returns. Risk factors: US tech valuations (40–70x PE), regulatory antitrust risk, US-China tech decoupling, and currency hedging costs (~2% annualised). Best suited for long-term investors seeking US technology exposure without a US brokerage account. SEBI's $7B overseas ETF limit for Indian mutual funds constrains fresh inflows; funds temporarily close subscriptions when limit is breached.

name: Mirae Asset Hang Seng TECH ETF

index.name: Hang Seng Tech Index
index.methodology: Free-Float Market Cap, max 8% per stock
index.description: Hang Seng Tech Index tracks the 30 largest technology companies listed in Hong Kong — China's equivalent of the Nasdaq. This index offers Indian investors a rare regulated window into Chinese internet giants (Alibaba, Tencent, Meituan) and hardware champions (Xiaomi, BYD, SMIC) that are otherwise inaccessible via Indian domestic markets. The index plunged 65% from its 2021 peak following Beijing's tech crackdown (Ant Group IPO ban, Didi delisting, education sector wipeout), creating an asymmetric recovery opportunity — these companies trade at 10–15x PE vs 25–35x for US equivalents. Structural tailwind: China's 1.4B consumer digital adoption is ~3 years behind the US on cloud migration, e-commerce penetration in Tier-3/4 cities, and AI-native enterprise software. Key risks: regulatory unpredictability (overnight policy reversals), VIE structure (Indian investors own Cayman SPVs, not direct Chinese equity), US-China geopolitical tensions, and Taiwan Strait risk premium. The HKD-INR currency trajectory adds another return layer — HKD is pegged to USD so any USD strength amplifies rupee-denominated returns.

name: Nippon India ETF Hang Seng BeES

index.name: Hang Seng Index
index.methodology: Free-Float Market Cap, max 10% per stock
index.description: Hang Seng Index (HSI) is Hong Kong's benchmark equity index — 80 companies across Finance, Properties, Utilities, Commerce & Industry. Unlike MAHKTECH's pure China internet focus, HSI is a diversified blend: AIA (life insurance), HSBC (global banking), HKEX (stock exchange), Hong Kong Utilities, Chinese banks (BoC HK) and property developers alongside internet giants Tencent and Alibaba. This makes HNGSNGBEES a more conservative, less volatile International ETF than MAHKTECH — suitable for investors wanting broad Hong Kong + China blue-chip exposure rather than concentrated tech bets. Index carries unique characteristics: HKD peg to USD means no HKD-USD currency risk but full USD-INR exposure. HSI was the world's worst-performing major index in 2021–2022 (down 40%) following China's regulatory crackdown and property sector debt crisis (Evergrande). A mean-reversion thesis drives continued interest — the index trades at 9-10x PE vs 21x for S&P 500 and 23x for Nifty 50. Risks: China property sector overhang (Country Garden, Evergrande), capital outflow pressure, Hong Kong autonomy concerns post-2019 political changes, and global interest rate impact on REIT/utility components.

index.name: NASDAQ 100 Index
index.description: MON100 (Motilal Oswal NASDAQ 100 ETF) was India's first international ETF and remains the most popular Nasdaq 100 product available to Indian retail investors — with ₹10,200 Cr+ AUM it is by far the largest US-tracking fund in India. The NASDAQ 100 Index selects the 100 largest non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market by modified market cap — effectively capturing the world's leading technology, consumer internet, biotech and media companies. The index's extraordinary performance history (+20% CAGR over 10 years in USD), amplified by INR depreciation against USD, has delivered 22-25% CAGR in INR terms to patient Indian investors. MON100 gives Indian investors genuine global diversification: exposure to Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Amazon, Meta, Alphabet — companies whose products Indians use daily but couldn't previously invest in. The vehicle eliminates forex account requirements (LRS/FEMA limits), works within standard ₹50 lakh LRS quota, attracts long-term capital gains tax at 20% indexation post 36 months. Tracking error vs benchmark is typically 0.5-1% annually. Currency risk (INR/USD) historically works in Indian investors' favour as rupee has depreciated 3-4% annually against dollar — boosting returns. Ideal as 10-20% of a long-term equity portfolio for geographic diversification.
